signupData.goalsElaboration: Competitive cyclist (Cat 3 road racer), strength training purely for cycling performance and injury prevention. Peak racing season in 10 weeks. Minimal effective dose approach.
signupData.locationElaboration: Basement home gym - barbell, plates up to 300 lb, squat rack, bench
signupData.injuries: Tight hip flexors, previous lower back strain from poor bike fit (resolved). Avoids leg volume that makes legs heavy for cycling.
signupData.experienceElaboration: 12 years competitive cycling, 4 years strength training. FTP 315 watts. Doesn't care about strength PRs, only cycling performance. Trains Tue/Fri at 6 PM after recovery rides, 40-50 min.

onboardingMessages: I'm a competitive road cyclist, Cat 3. I use strength training purely to support my cycling and prevent injuries. Racing season starts in 10 weeks.
I have a home gym in my basement with a barbell, squat rack, bench, and plates up to 300 lb. I train Tuesday and Friday, 40-50 minutes.
I need minimal effective dose - can't have heavy legs for my rides. I don't care about strength PRs, just cycling performance and durability.
